name: blogwatcher
tagline: ClawHub (72★): Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds for updates using the blogwatcher CLI.
verdict: Quality is the weak spot: the skill never says what to do when the binary is missing, a feed fetch fails, or a blog isn't found, leaving agents guessing on any hiccup. It also lacks scope boundaries and version tracking, though security is clean.

highlights.summary: No mention anywhere of what to do if the binary is missing/not installed, a feed fetch fails, or a blog isn't found — total silence on failure across the install/scan flow.
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in description or body; skill has multiple subcommands so misfire on adjacent tasks (e.g. general web scrapin
No version field, changelog section, or dated revision notes anywhere in frontmatter or body.
